questions.body:
Which one of the following statements is INCORRECT?
(A) If the net force on a body is zero, its velocity is constant or zero
(B) If the net force on a body is zero, its acceleration is constant and non-zero
(C) If the velocity of a body is constant, its acceleration is zero
(D) A body may have a varying velocity yet a constant speed

choices.a: If the net force on a body is zero, its velocity is constant or zero
choices.b: If the net force on a body is zero, its acceleration is constant and non-zero
choices.c: If the velocity of a body is constant, its acceleration is zero
choices.d: A body may have a varying velocity yet a constant speed

questions.subject: physics

questions.body: Two forces each of magnitude P act on a body placed at a point O as shown. The force necessary to keep the body at rest is of magnitude.
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: |F₁| = |F₂| angle between F₁ and F₂ is 120° resultant will be P along x-axis. Force necessary to keep the body resist will be P in -x direction

questions.body: Two spheres having masses 10 g and 25 g are projected horizontally from the same height with velocities v₁ and v₂ and they fall to the ground in time intervals t₁ and t₂ respectively. If the ratio v₁ : v₂ is 1 : 3, the ratio t₁ : t₂ will be

questions.solution: t = sqrt(2h/g), here h is same, so t₁, t₂ = 1 : 1

questions.body: A convex lens L₁ forms an image of the same size as that of the object at a distance of 24 cm. If the lens L₁ is replaced by another convex lens L₂, the image formed is magnified and erect. Therefore, the focal length of L₂ is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Image formed by lens L₁ is of same size so 2f₁ = 24 cm ⟹ v for second lens L₂, u = -24 cm, image is magnified and erect it is only possible when object is between pole and F, so focal length of L₂ will be greater than 24 cm.

questions.body: A wooden ball of density 0.8 g/cm³ is placed in water. The ratio of the volume above the water surface to that below the water surface is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Total volume of solid = v₁ Density of solid, D = 0.8 g/cm³ emerged volume of solid = v₂ Density of water, d = 1 g/cm³ ∴ v₁/V = D/d ⟹ V/v₁ = d/V = 1 ⟹ V-v₁/v₁ = d-D/D = Volume outside water/Volume insidewater = (d-D)/D = (1-0.8)/0.8 = 0.25

questions.body: a converging beam of light falls on a convex mirror of radius of curvature 20 cm, the point of convergence being 10 cm behind the mirror. The image is

choices.a: virtual and formed 10 cm in front of the mirror
choices.b: real and formed in front of the mirror
choices.d: virtual and formed 10 cm behind the mirror

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Converging point is behind the mirror, u = +10 cm f = 10 cm ∴ 1/f = 1/v + 1/u = 1/v + 1/10 ⟹ 1/v = 1/10 - 1/10 ⟹ V = ∞ image will be at ∞

questions.body: When 2.8 g of nitrogen reacts with 0.6 g of hydrogen, 3.4 g of ammonia gas is produced. If 5.8 g of nitrogen is allowed to react with 0.6 g of hydrogen, then the amount of ammonia produced is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃
1 mole 3 mole 2 mole
5.6/28 = 0.2, 0.6/6 = 0.3
0.2/1 = 0.2, 0.3/3 = 0.1
means H₂ is L.R.
6 g H₂ gives 34g of NH₃
0.6g H₂ gives = 34 × 0.6/6 × 1 = 3.4 g

questions.body: A number of electric bulbs of rating 220 volt, 100 watt are to be connected in parallel to a 220 volt supply. If a 5 A fuse wire is used for this arrangement to bulbs, the maximum number of bulbs that can be included in the arrangement will be
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Current drawn by each bulb,
I = P/V = 100/220 = 5/11 A
Now number of bulb is n, so
n × 5/11 = 5 ⇒ n = 11 bulbs

questions.body: In the circuit given below, AB is a thick copper wire and S is a switch. When the switch is closed, the effective resistance of the circuit will be
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: If switch is closed then no current will flow through 1Ω resistance, hence equivalent resistance is 3Ω

questions.body: In figure (1) ammeter reads I1 and voltmeter reads V1. Similarly, in figure (2) ammeter reads I2 while voltmeter reads V2. Then which of the following statements is correct?
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
In first figure Ra = R₁ + R₂
In second figure Rp = R₁R₂/(R₁ + R₂) which is
less than Ra
I₁ = E/(Ra + r), I₂ = E/(Rp + r),
SO, I₂ > I₁
V₁ = ERa/(Ra + r) = E/(1 + (r/Ra)), V₂ = E/(1 + (r/Rp))
So, V₁ > V₂
Answer (B)

questions.body: If the altitudes of a triangle are in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4, then the lengths of the corresponding sides are in the ratio:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let AD = h₁ BE = h₂
CF = h₃
Area of ΔABC = 1/2 ah₁ = 1/2 bh₂ = 1/2 ch₃
⇒ ah₁ = bh₂ = ch₃
Given : h₁ : h₂ : h₃ = 2 : 3 : 4
⇒ ∴ h₁ = 2k, h₂ = 3k and h₃ = 4k
So, a(2k) = b(3k) = c(4k)
2a = 3b = 4c
Dividing by 12
⇒ a/6 = b/4 = c/3.

questions.body: In case of a right circular cylinder the radius of base and height are in the ratio 2 : 3. Therefore, the ratio of lateral surface area to the total surface area is:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Given, r : h = 2 : 3
So, r = 2k, h = 3k
∴ LSA/TSA = 2πrh/(2πr(h + r))
= h/(h + r) = 3k/(3k + 2k) = 3k/5k = 3:5

questions.body: Number of molecule present in a given amount of CO2 and O2 is the same at STP. If the weight of CO2 is 5.5g, then the weight of O2 is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
If the no. of moles are same then no. of molecules also same.
nCO₂ = 5.5/44
= 0.125 mole
1 mole of O₂ = 32 g
0. 125 mole of O₂ = 32 × 0.125 = 4g

questions.body: If sin θ + cosec θ = 2, then [sin^8 θ + cosec^8 θ] will have the value
questions.subject: maths

sin²θ + cosec²θ = 4 - 2 = 2
Similarly,
sin⁴θ + cosec⁴θ
= 2 and sin⁶θ + cosec⁶θ = 2

questions.subject: chemistry

mass of 1 molecules of phosphorus

questions.body: All the three sides of a △ ABC have lengths in integral units, with AB = 2001 units and BC = 1002 = units. The possible number of triangles with this condition is:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let the third side of triangle be x unit
So, x + 1002 > 2001
x > 999
Also, x < 2001 + 1002
x < 3003
∴ 999 < x < 3003
So, possible no. of triangles
= 3003 - 999 - 1 = 2003.

questions.body: The graph of the equation y = 2x^2 + 4x + 3 has its lowest point at:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
y = 2x² + 4x + 3
y = 2[x² + 2x + (3/2)]
y = 2[(x + 1)² - 1 + (3/2)]
y = 2[(x + 1)² + (1/2)]
∴ Lowest point will be at x = -1, y = 1
So, lowest point (-1, 1).

questions.body: If a, b, c are in continued proportion, the expression (a^2 + ab + b^2)/(b^2 + bc + c^2) can be simplified to:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
a, b, c are in continued proportion.
∴ a/b = b/c = k
a = bk, b = ck and a = ck²
a² + ab + b² = b²k² + b²k + b² = b²(k² + k + 1)
b² + bc + c² = c²k² + c²k + c² = c²(k² + k + 1)
= b²/c² = k² = a/c = k²

questions.body: AD, BE and CF are the medians of △ABC. The sum of lengths of segments BE and CF is:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
By mid point theorem
EF = (1/2) BC
In ΔBGC
BG + GC > BC .....(i)
In ΔGFE
FG + GE > FE .....(ii)
Add (i) and (ii) we get
BE + CF > BC + FE
BE + CF > BC + BC/2 [FE = BC/2]
BE + CF > 3BC/2

questions.body: In the figure shown, chord ED is parallel to diameter AC of a circle. If ∠CBE = 60°, then ∠DEC must be:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
∠BAC = ∠EBC = 60° [Angles on the same segment of circle]
∠AEC = 90° [Angles in a semicircle]
∴ In Δ AEC
∠ ACE = 180° - (60° + 90°) = 30°
Now, ∠ CBD = ∠ ACE = 30°

questions.body: If a² + b² + c² = 1 and p = ab + bc + ca, then
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
We know AM ≥ GM
(a+b)/2 ≥ √ab
a + b ≥ 2√ab
Squaring both sides
a² + b² + 2ab ≥ 4 ab
a² + b² ≥ 2 ab
So, b² + c² ≥ 2bc
c² + a² ≥ 2ac
By adding (i), (ii), (iii)
2(a² + b² + c²) ≥ 2(ab + bc + ca)
2(1) ≥ 2(ab + bc + ca)
1 ≥ P
And (a + b + c)² ≥ 0
a² + b² + c² + 2(ab + bc + ca ) ≥ 0
1 + 2 P ≥ 0
P ≥ -1/2
From (iv) & (v)
-1/2 ≤ P ≤ 1

questions.body: If x^2 – 5x + 1 = 0, then (x^10 + 1)/x^5 has the value
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
ar ΔOAB + ar ΔOBC + ar ΔOCA = ar Δ ABC
1/2×1×a + 1/2×1×b + 1/2×1×10 = 1/2 ab
a +b + 10 = ab
perimeter = ab
a + b = ab - 10
Squaring both sides
a² + b² + 2ab = a²b² + 100 - 20 ab
100 + 22ab = a²b² + 100
a²b² = 22 ab
∴ ab = 22
So, perimeter = 22 cm.

x² - 5x + 1 = 0
x² + 1 = 5x
x + 1/x = 5
To find :
x¹⁰ + 1/x⁵ = x⁵ + 1/x⁵
(x + 1/x)⁵ = ⁵C₀ x⁵ + ⁵C₁ x⁴ · 1/x + ⁵C₂ x³
x · 1/x² + ⁵C₃ x x² · 1/x³ + ⁵C₄ x
x · 1/x⁴ + ⁵C₅ · 1/x⁵
= x⁵ + 5x³ + 10x + 10/x + 5/x³ + 1/x⁵
(5)⁵ = (x⁵ + 1/x⁵) + 5(x³ + 1/x³) + 10(x + 1/x)
3125 = x⁵ + 1/x⁵ + 5
[(x + 1/x)³ - 3(x + 1/x)] + 10(5)
3125 - 50 - 5 [(5)³ - 3(5)] = x⁵ + 1/x⁵
∴ x⁵ + 1/x⁵ = 3125 - 50 - 550
x⁵ + 1/x⁵ = 2525.

questions.body: The area of the circle is double when its radius r is increased by a. Therefore, radius r equals.
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Let the radius of circle is r. Then, new radius = r + a ∴ 2πr² = π (r + a)² 2r² = (r + a)² (√2r)² = (r+a)² r = a/(√2-1) r = (√2+1)a

questions.body: The product of the roots of the equation 3√(5 + x) + 3√(5 – x) = 1 is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: √8+x + √8-x = 1 √8+x = 1-√8-x Cubing both sides 8 + x = 1 - 8 + x - 3 ∛8-x (1-√8+x) 15 = -3 ∛8-x (√8-x) -5 = ∛64-x² Cubing both sides -125 = 64-x² x² - 189 = 0 Product of roots = -189.

questions.body: A point P is outside a circle at a distance of 13 cm from its centre. A secant from P cuts the circle in Q and R such that QR = 7 cm and the segment PQ of the secant exterior to the circle is 9 cm. Therefore, the radius of circle is :
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: OP = 13 PT = 13 - r PS = 13 + r PQ = 9 PR = 16 As we know PT × PS = PQ × PR (13 - r)(13 + r) = 9 × 16 169 - r² = 144 r² = 25 r = 5 cm.

questions.body: In the figure shown AB is parallel to DE. The difference between angles x and y is :
questions.subject: maths
questions.skip_reason: Cannot solve without the geometric figure showing angles x and y
questions.solution: ∠1 = 35° [Alternate interior Angles] ∴ x = 180 - (53 + 35) = 92° y = 180 - x [Linear pair] = 180 - 92 = 88 ∴ x - y = 92° - 88° = 4°
